Title: A cis-carotene derived apocarotenoid regulates etioplast and chloroplast development

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Etiolated cotyledons; newly emerged juvenile leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Retrograde signal controlling prolamellar body formation, etioplast development, chloroplast biogenesis, and seedling greening
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CRTISO/CCR2 causes accumulation of specific cis-carotenes and production of an apocarotenoid signal that suppresses normal plastid development, causing loss of PLB and leaf virescence under short photoperiods
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function becomes limiting in dark/short-photoperiod developing tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (ccr2); photoperiod shift assays; carotenoid HPLC profiling; TEM of etioplasts/chloroplasts; de-etiolation greening assay; RNA-seq; chemical inhibition with D15; genetic suppression by ziso and det1
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Short photoperiod; extended darkness; etiolation; de-etiolation; D15 treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ccr2 mutants accumulated specific cis-carotenes in dark-grown and short-photoperiod tissues. 2. ccr2 lacked prolamellar bodies despite near-normal POR abundance. 3. Genetic or chemical suppression of downstream cis-carotene/apocarotenoid formation restored PLB formation and greening, indicating CCR2 normally prevents generation of a plastid-development-inhibitory apocarotenoid signal.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA498324; DNA-seq/NGS mapping accession NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating CRTISO activity and dark-period-dependent cis-carotene accumulation could tune plastid development and carotenoid signaling, but may cause virescence under short photoperiods
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Leaf virescence; delayed greening; impaired chloroplast ultrastructure under extended darkness
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study infers that the decisive product is not the cis-carotene itself alone but a derived unknown apocarotenoid signal generated in the ccr2 background.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Retrograde regulator of PLB formation and plastid development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ZISO in the ccr2 background blocks formation of downstream cis-carotenes and restores PLB formation, cotyledon greening, and leaf plastid development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Second-site mutant mapping of ziso-155; complementation with ZISO overexpression; double mutant analysis with ziso1-4; carotenoid profiling; TEM; de-etiolation chlorophyll assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early cis-carotene isomerization segment upstream of neurosporene and tetra-cis-lycopene formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Downstream cis-carotenes enabling formation of di-cis-ζ-carotene, neurosporene isomers and tetra-cis-lycopene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes isomerization of tri-cis-ζ-carotene in the carotenoid pathway, enabling production of more downstream cis-carotene intermediates in darkness
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for formation of the apocarotenoid-generating cis-carotenes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in dark carotenoid isomerization for production of specific cis-carotene precursors linked to ACS formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ziso-155 was identified as a suppressor of ccr2 virescence. 2. ziso loss prevented accumulation of neurosporene isomers, tetra-cis-lycopene and di-cis-ζ-carotene in ccr2. 3. Blocking these downstream cis-carotenes restored PLB formation and chloroplast development, implicating them or their cleavage products as causal signals.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ZISO editing can modulate dark-dependent cis-carotene flux and apocarotenoid signaling affecting plastid development
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ziso alone causes delayed greening/slight virescence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ziso-155 introduces a premature stop codon producing a truncated 212 aa protein instead of 367 aa.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DET1 family; nuclear repressor of photomorphogenesis; exon 4 contains six-hairpin glycosidase-like domain homology (IPR008928)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cis-carotene-derived apocarotenoid signal (ACS) / plastid-development outcome affecting chlorophyll accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly supports chlorophyll accumulation and normal plastid differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Plastid development; regulation of chlorophyll biosynthesis capacity and photosynthesis-associated nuclear gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-transcriptional regulation of PIF3/HY5 protein levels; negative regulation of photomorphogenesis; in mutant background affects PORA transcriptional repression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: det1-154 suppresses ccr2 virescence and restores PLB formation and greening while reducing levels of specific downstream cis-carotenes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PORA; PIF3; HY5; PhANGs including LHCB2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: DET1 normally maintains high PIF3 and low HY5 protein levels and represses photomorphogenesis; det1-154 lowers full-length DET1 function, enabling POR restoration in ccr2 by ACS
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with COP1 and DDB1/chromatin regulatory machinery
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A parallel decisive controller of PLB formation and chloroplast development that genetically interacts with ACS signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Suppressor mapping of det1-154; cDNA splice analysis; qRT-PCR; transgenic complementation with DET1-OE; carotenoid profiling; TEM; de-etiolation assays; western blots for DET1/POR/PIF3/HY5
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark growth; short photoperiod; de-etiolation; D15 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. det1-154 was identified as a ccr2 suppressor restoring plastid development. 2. det1-154 reduced specific downstream cis-carotenes in ccr2 and restored PLB formation and seedling greening. 3. ACS can override det1-mediated PORA repression, indicating parallel pathways controlling plastid development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting DET1-related light signaling can reshape carotenoid-associated plastid development, but strong pleiotropy limits deployment
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Smaller rosette; shorter floral stem; reduced fertility; constitutive photomorphogenic traits
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for DET1 overexpression complementation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: det1-154 is an alternatively spliced allele lacking exon 4, producing a shorter 521 aa protein.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Chlorophyll / photosynthesis-associated gene output downstream of ACS signaling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports regulation of chlorophyll accumulation and chloroplast development indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photomorphogenesis-linked regulation of photosynthesis-associated nuclear genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of light-responsive development; ACS elevates PIF3 transcript and protein levels
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in ccr2 etiolated and de-etiolated seedlings and juvenile leaves; reduced by D15
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated PIF3 in ccr2 correlates with reduced LHCB2/PhANG expression and delayed greening; D15 or ziso suppression lowers PIF3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses PhANG expression when elevated relative to HY5
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HY5 (antagonistic regulatory hub); phytochromes (stated by gene annotation)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the key PIF3/HY5 hub through which ACS delays chloroplast development and greening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; western blot; D15 treatment comparisons; genetic comparison with det1 and ziso suppressors
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolation; de-etiolation; short photoperiod; D15 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF3 transcript and protein accumulated strongly in ccr2. 2. D15 reversed this increase, showing dependence on carotenoid cleavage. 3. Elevated PIF3 contributes to higher PIF3/HY5 ratio and repression of PhANG expression during plastid development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating PIF3 may tune plastid developmental responses to carotenoid-derived signals, but broad light-signaling pleiotropy is expected
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats PIF3 as a regulated output of ACS rather than the primary discovered causal mutation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Chlorophyll / photosynthesis-associated gene output downstream of ACS signaling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly supports chlorophyll accumulation and chloroplast differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photomorphogenesis-linked activation of photosynthesis-associated nuclear genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of light-responsive genes; ACS represses HY5 transcript and protein accumulation in ccr2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in ccr2 etiolated and de-etiolated seedlings and juvenile leaves; restored/increased by D15
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced HY5 in ccr2 correlates with lower LHCB2 expression and delayed greening; D15 restores HY5 levels
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Counterbalances PIF3 in the core hub through which ACS modulates chloroplast development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; western blot; D15 treatment comparisons; genetic comparison with det1 and ziso suppressors
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolation; de-etiolation; short photoperiod; D15 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 transcript and protein were reduced in ccr2. 2. D15 restored HY5 to WT/det1-like levels, demonstrating dependence on carotenoid cleavage. 3. HY5 reduction contributes to a high PIF3/HY5 ratio associated with reduced PhANG expression and delayed plastid development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: HY5 is a potential leverage point for improving greening responses, but major pleiotropic light-signaling effects are likely
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad changes in photomorphogenesis and stress/light responses
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Like PIF3, HY5 is a mechanistic node transcriptionally regulated by the inferred apocarotenoid signal.
